channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: India's BrahMos missile gains global traction as defense exports expand
item.description: Indonesia's decision to procure India's BrahMos supersonic cruise missile marks a significant milestone for New Delhi's defense exports. The missile, known for its speed and precision, has attracted i...

item.title: Weekly Event Guide: July 13-19 Highlights
item.description: This article serves as a comprehensive guide to cultural and entertainment events in Johannesburg and surrounding areas during the week of July 13-19, 2026. Key highlights include Disney’s The Lion Ki...

item.title: Philippine Military Academy Receives Funding for Modern Warfare Laboratory
item.description: The Philippine Military Academy (PMA) has secured a P379.48 million grant from the Bloomberry Cultural Foundation Inc. (BCFI), a CSR arm of Bloomberry Resorts Corp, to establish a modern warfare labor...

item.title: Milly Chebby Shares Weight Loss Journey and Defends Herself Against Online Criticism
item.description: Veteran comedian Terence Creative's wife, content creator Milly Chebby, has shared a TikTok video showcasing her weight loss transformation while addressing online critics who labeled her a 'marriage ...

item.title: Misconceptions About Nutrition in Diabetes and Hypertension Patients
item.description: Healthcare professionals are warning that many patients with diabetes and hypertension have incorrect beliefs about their diets, which can worsen their conditions. These misconceptions, like avoiding ...

item.title: Security Forces Kill 88 Militants in Balochistan's Operation Shaban
item.description: Pakistan's security forces have killed 88 militants belonging to India's proxy group Fitna al-Khawarij during Operation Shaban in Balochistan since July 5, according to official sources. The joint ope...

item.title: Bournemouth's drug crisis: Violence, public indecency and local authorities' response to escalating social issues
item.description: The article details the growing social and public order challenges in Bournemouth, highlighting incidents of drug use, violence, and inappropriate behaviour in public spaces. Journalist Neil Sears rec...

item.title: Neil the Seal returns to the ocean after annual moult, but his return is vital for survival
item.description: Neil the Seal, Tasmania's beloved celebrity, has completed his annual moult and returned to the ocean, ending his 'reign of terror' on land. The five-year-old elephant seal's solo return to his birthp...

item.title: Canadians Eligible for $8M in Beef Price-Fixing Class-Action Settlement
item.description: A class-action lawsuit against major beef producers in Canada has reached a settlement worth nearly $8 million, with JBS and National Beef agreeing to pay $7.498 million and $495,000 respectively to a...

item.title: Spain's Mikel Merino Scores Late Goals in Consecutive World Cup Matches
item.description: Spain's Mikel Merino, the 'super sub' of the 2026 FIFA World Cup, secured a dramatic late victory against Belgium in the quarter-finals, marking his second consecutive last-minute goal in the tourname...

item.title: India and New Zealand Upgrade Ties to Strategic Partnership, Aim for ₹35,000 Crore Trade by 2030
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi and New Zealand Prime Minister Christopher Luxon have elevated bilateral ties to a strategic partnership, setting a five-year target to double annual trade in goods and se...

item.title: Kirinyaga University Graduate Secures Job After Comedian's Generous Offer
item.description: A Kirinyaga University graduate who had been sleeping in a roadside kibanda has experienced a life-changing transformation after celebrated comedian Terence Creative offered him a job. The story, shar...

item.title: Remembering Ann Widdecombe: A Political Icon's Legacy
item.description: The article reflects on the life and legacy of Ann Widdecombe, a prominent British politician known for her sharp intellect, charisma, and unflinching honesty. Following the murder investigation into ...

item.title: Trump's Removal of Election Officials Sparks Concerns Over U.S. Midterm Elections
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has faced accusations of attempting to influence the upcoming midterm elections after firing the last two Democratic members of the bipartisan Election Assistance Commissio...

item.title: Pakistan to Present Evidence of India's Involvement in Balochistan Proxy War at Global Forums
item.description: The Pakistani government has decided to present evidence of India's alleged involvement in Balochistan as a 'proxy war' at international forums, according to Prime Minister's Adviser Rana Sanaullah. S...

item.title: Tanzania Introduces AI-Powered Parking System to Combat Fraud and Improve Efficiency

item.title: Lawyer Bodies Stand Firm on Blue Silk Rank for Senior Counsel Despite Court Ruling

item.title: UK-based lawyer renounces Nigerian citizenship, claims country hasn't offered him anything
item.description: UK-based Nigerian lawyer Baron Chymaker has applied to renounce his Nigerian citizenship, claiming the country hasn't given him anything despite his investments. The decision has sparked online debate...

item.title: DOH Secretary Ted Herbosa Undergoes Knee Replacement Surgery and Recovery
item.description: Health Secretary Teodoro 'Ted' Herbosa has been on sick leave since July 6, 2026, for a knee replacement surgery. Health Undersecretary Albert Domingo confirmed the procedure was successful, and Herbo...

item.title: Trump administration explored bypassing election rules and fired EAC commissioners
item.description: The Trump administration considered methods to circumvent Election Assistance Commission (EAC) guidelines for state voting machines, according to a Reuters report. Four anonymous sources revealed that...
